name: Coruscant
desc: Galactic capital. Seat of the Empire since Palpatine's reorganization. The Jedi Temple — now the Imperial Palace — dominates the skyline. In 16 BBY the Imperial Security Bureau operates from here, and the Senate still convenes as a rubber-stamp body.

name: Corellia
desc: Shipbuilding powerhouse. CEC (Corellian Engineering Corporation) churns out CR90 corvettes and YT-series freighters under Imperial contract. CorSec maintains a tenuous independence. Smugglers call it home.

name: Kuat
desc: Home of Kuat Drive Yards — the Empire's primary Star Destroyer manufacturer. The orbital ring is visible from hyperspace. In 16 BBY, KDY is producing Imperial-class Star Destroyers at maximum capacity.

name: Alderaan
desc: A world of art, diplomacy, and quiet resistance. Bail Organa leads the planet and secretly funds rebel cells. In 16 BBY, Alderaan still stands — beautiful, defiant, and doomed.

name: Chandrila
desc: Mon Mothma's homeworld. Agrarian, politically active, and increasingly watched by Imperial Intelligence. In 16 BBY, Mon Mothma still serves in the Imperial Senate while building the Alliance in secret.

name: Hosnian Prime
desc: A cosmopolitan Core World. Busy trade hub with significant military shipyards. Unremarkable in 16 BBY — decades from its future significance.

name: Duro
desc: Polluted industrial world, surface abandoned. Orbital cities house the Duros population. Major trade route junction on the Corellian Trade Spine.

name: Nubia
desc: Starship engine manufacturer. Nubian Design Collective produces elegant drives favored by diplomats and smugglers alike. Connected to Corellia by the Corellian Run.

name: Naboo
desc: Palpatine's homeworld. Lush, peaceful on the surface. The Empire treats it as a trophy — heavily garrisoned and loyal. Theed remains beautiful. The Gungans keep to the deep.

name: Kashyyyk
desc: Wookiee homeworld. Under Imperial occupation since Order 66. Wookiees are enslaved and transported offworld for labor. Trandoshan slavers operate with Imperial sanction. The wroshyr trees still grow.

name: Bothawui
desc: Bothan homeworld. The Bothan Spynet operates from here — the galaxy's premier intelligence network. In 16 BBY, the Bothans play all sides and sell to the highest bidder.

name: Takodana
desc: Lush forest world. Maz Kanata's castle on the shore of Nymeve Lake is a neutral ground for pirates, smugglers, and travelers. Varga the Hutt controls the far shore. Campaign-critical location.

name: Onderon
desc: Jungle world with a history of rebellion. Saw Gerrera trained partisans here during the Clone Wars. In 16 BBY, the seeds of organized resistance are already planted in the highlands.

name: Mandalore
desc: Devastated by the Empire's Great Purge. In 16 BBY, the Purge hasn't happened yet — Mandalore exists under uneasy Imperial occupation. Bo-Katan Kryze leads a fractured resistance.

name: Nal Hutta
desc: Hutt homeworld. Swampy, polluted, opulent in the palatial districts. The Hutt Grand Council operates openly — the Empire tolerates them as useful intermediaries. In 16 BBY, the Hutts still command their own territory.

name: Nar Shaddaa
desc: The Smuggler's Moon. Nal Hutta's vertical cityscape moon — neon, vice, and violence stacked a thousand levels deep. Every criminal enterprise in the galaxy has an office here.

name: Ord Mantell
desc: Scrapyard world and bounty hunter haven. Junkyards stretch to the horizon. In 16 BBY, it's a place where people go to disappear — or to find people who disappeared.

name: Bespin
desc: Gas giant. Cloud City floats in the upper atmosphere — a tibanna gas mining operation. In 16 BBY, Lando Calrissian has NOT yet arrived. The city is run by a succession of administrators and mining guilds.

name: Hoth
desc: Ice world. Uninhabited, unmapped, and brutally cold. In 16 BBY, no one has any reason to go here. Yet.

name: Dagobah
desc: Swamp world. Strong in the Force — the dark side cave masks all signatures. In 16 BBY, Yoda has been in exile here for three years. No one knows.

name: Endor
desc: Forest moon. Ewok homeworld. In 16 BBY, the second Death Star hasn't been conceived. But the Ebon Spire — Revan's ancient forward base — sits deep in the Dead Forest, waiting. Campaign-critical location.

name: Jakku
desc: Desert world. The Burning Deck cantina sits in Cratertown — a settlement built in the shadow of a crashed Separatist cruiser. This is where the crew met Maya. Campaign starting location.

name: Tatooine
desc: Twin-sunned desert world. Jabba the Hutt rules from his palace. Mos Eisley and Mos Espa serve as spaceports for smugglers and moisture farmers. In 16 BBY, a boy named Luke is six years old on a moisture farm.

name: Geonosis
desc: Rocky desert world. Site of the first Clone Wars battle. In 16 BBY, the Empire has sterilized the planet — the Geonosian population has been nearly exterminated to protect Death Star construction secrets.

name: Mustafar
desc: Volcanic hellscape. Vader's fortress sits on the obsidian shore. In 16 BBY, Fortress Vader is under construction — a black tower rising from the lava fields, built over an ancient Sith shrine.

name: Scarif
desc: Tropical paradise hiding an Imperial data vault. The planetary shield gate controls all access. In 16 BBY, the citadel tower stores the Empire's most classified projects — including the Death Star plans.

name: Lothal
desc: Agricultural world under heavy Imperial industrialization. In 16 BBY, the Empire is strip-mining the planet and building TIE fighter factories. A ghost crew hasn't started fighting back yet.

name: Ryloth
desc: Twi'lek homeworld. Tidally locked — one face burns, one freezes. The Empire uses Twi'leks as slave labor. Cham Syndulla's Free Ryloth Movement fights a guerrilla war in the mountains.

name: Mon Cala
desc: Ocean world. Home of the Mon Calamari and Quarren. In 16 BBY, the Empire occupies the planet. The Mon Calamari are building warships in secret — the fleet that will become the backbone of the Rebel Alliance.

name: Dathomir
desc: Dark world of witchcraft and rancors. The Nightsisters were nearly exterminated by Grievous during the Clone Wars. In 16 BBY, Mother Talzin is dead, but the Nightsister traditions survive in hiding.

name: Felucia
desc: Fungal jungle world. Aayla Secura was killed here during Order 66. In 16 BBY, the Empire maintains a small garrison. The jungle has already consumed most Clone Wars-era battlefields.

name: Kessel
desc: Spice mining world. The Kessel Run — a dangerous hyperspace route through the Maw — connects it to the galaxy. In 16 BBY, the mines are worked by slave labor under Imperial supervision.

name: Ilum
desc: Ice world. Site of the Jedi kyber crystal caves. In 16 BBY, the Empire is mining kyber crystals — the essential component for the Death Star's superlaser. The caves are being hollowed out.

name: Kamino
desc: Ocean world beyond the Outer Rim. The clone facilities on Tipoca City have been shut down — the Empire switched to conscription. In 16 BBY, Kamino is abandoned and erased from official charts.

name: Sullust
desc: Volcanic world. SoroSuub Corporation dominates the economy. The Sullustans will eventually ally with the Rebellion. In 16 BBY, SoroSuub cooperates with the Empire — reluctantly.

name: Rodia
desc: Swampy jungle world. Rodian homeworld. Known for producing bounty hunters and gunslingers. The Empire maintains trade agreements with the Rodian Grand Protector.

name: Fondor
desc: Industrial shipyard world. Fondor Shipyards produce warships for the Empire. In 16 BBY, the yards are expanding to meet the demand for the growing Imperial fleet.

name: Eriadu
desc: Tarkin's homeworld. Industrial, politically influential. Grand Moff Tarkin rules the Outer Rim from here. In 16 BBY, Tarkin is consolidating power and pushing for the Death Star project.

name: Christophsis
desc: Crystal world. Major Clone Wars battlefield. In 16 BBY, the crystal cities are being rebuilt under Imperial administration. The planet's strategic value lies in its rare crystal exports.

name: Utapau
desc: Sinkhole world. Obi-Wan Kenobi defeated Grievous here. In 16 BBY, the Pau'ans and Utai live in the sinkhole cities under light Imperial presence. The sinkholes go deeper than anyone has mapped.

name: Mygeeto
desc: Frozen crystalline world. Major banking clan stronghold during the Clone Wars. Ki-Adi-Mundi was killed here during Order 66. The Empire controls the planet's valuable crystal reserves.

name: Cato Neimoidia
desc: Bridge city world. Former Trade Federation stronghold. Plo Koon was shot down here during Order 66. In 16 BBY, the Neimoidians are distrusted by the Empire and stripped of their former power.

name: Dantooine
desc: Remote grassland world. The original Jedi Enclave ruins stand abandoned. In 16 BBY, a small rebel cell uses the planet's remoteness as cover. The Empire considers it too insignificant to garrison.

name: Yavin 4
desc: Jungle moon orbiting the gas giant Yavin. Ancient Massassi temples rise above the canopy. In 16 BBY, the temples are abandoned. The Rebel Alliance hasn't found this place yet.

name: Jedha
desc: Holy city world. Sacred to the Church of the Force and the Guardians of the Whills. In 16 BBY, the Empire is occupying Jedha and mining its kyber crystals. The Holy City still stands — for now.

name: Eadu
desc: Storm-lashed research world. In 16 BBY, Galen Erso is here — forced to work on the Death Star's superlaser under Krennic's supervision. The research facility clings to rain-swept cliffs.

name: Wobani
desc: Imperial labor camp world. In 16 BBY, political prisoners and dissidents are worked to exhaustion in the quarries. A bleak, forgotten place.

name: Ajan Kloss
desc: Uncharted jungle moon. Dense canopy, bioluminescent undergrowth. The Vanishing Place — a repurposed pre-Republic temple — serves as Captain Skarn's mercenary base. Admiral Varth was held here. Campaign-critical location.

name: Rishi
desc: Remote listening post world at the edge of the Rishi Maze. During the Clone Wars, it housed a Republic outpost. The Rishi Maze — a dwarf satellite galaxy — is visible from the surface as a glowing smear across the night sky.

name: Corellia Run Junction
desc: Major hyperspace intersection where the Corellian Run crosses several secondary routes. A busy way-station for freighter traffic. Customs patrols are frequent but understaffed.

name: Malachor
desc: Ancient Sith world. A massive Sith temple lies beneath the surface, surrounded by petrified combatants from a battle thousands of years old. In 16 BBY, the planet is forgotten — a dead world hiding deadly secrets.

name: Pantora
desc: Moon of the ice world Orto Plutonia. Home of the Pantorans — blue-skinned near-humans with a strong senatorial tradition. Chairman Papanoida navigates Imperial politics carefully.

name: Zygerria
desc: Slaver world. The Zygerrian Slave Empire was supposedly disbanded by the Republic, but in 16 BBY the Empire turns a blind eye. The slave markets operate openly again.

name: Serenno
desc: Count Dooku's homeworld. Aristocratic, wealthy, and strategically located. After Dooku's death, the Empire seized his assets and installed a governor. The noble houses simmer with resentment.

name: Tython
desc: Ancient birthplace of the Jedi Order. In 16 BBY, Tython is lost — its coordinates erased from the Jedi Archives before Order 66. The Force is strong here, and the planet waits in silence.

name: Bogano
desc: Remote world of mesas and underground vaults. In 16 BBY, an ancient vault built by the Zeffo civilization lies sealed beneath the surface. Eno Cordova hid a Jedi holocron here.

name: Bracca
desc: Shipbreaking world. The Scrapper Guild dismantles Clone Wars-era warships under Imperial contract. Workers toil in dangerous conditions, cutting apart the Republic's legacy with plasma torches.

name: Nur
desc: Ocean moon. Fortress Inquisitorius — the headquarters of the Imperial Inquisition — is submerged beneath its dark waters. In 16 BBY, captured Force-sensitives are brought here. Few leave.

name: Zeffo
desc: Wind-swept tomb world of the ancient Zeffo civilization. Imperial archaeologists excavate the ruins, searching for artifacts connected to the Force. The tombs are deeper than they know.

name: Batuu
desc: Trading outpost on the edge of Wild Space. Black Spire Outpost serves smugglers, traders, and those who want to be forgotten. In 16 BBY, the Empire hasn't noticed this backwater yet.

name: Nevarro
desc: Volcanic world. A lava-scarred trading post where bounty hunters collect their pay. The Bounty Hunters' Guild operates a chapter house here. In 16 BBY, it's a lawless frontier town.

name: Arvala-7
desc: Remote desert world. Scattered settlements of Ugnaught vapor farmers. In 16 BBY, it's an unremarkable world on no major trade route — the kind of place empires forget exists.

name: Florrum
desc: Sulfurous wasteland. Hondo Ohnaka's pirate base during the Clone Wars. In 16 BBY, Hondo is still operating — diminished but unkillable. His compound is half-ruined and fully dangerous.

name: Vandor
desc: Frozen mountain world. The conveyex trains haul coaxium through treacherous mountain passes. In 16 BBY, the mining operations are under Imperial control.

name: Numidian Prime
desc: Forested world. In 16 BBY, a haven for smugglers running goods between Hutt Space and the Outer Rim. Low Imperial presence.
